title: ConvertKit vs Substack (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Substack: pricing, features, and which email platform suits creators best.
h1: ConvertKit vs Substack: Detailed Comparison for Creators
intro: ConvertKit and Substack are both popular email platforms for creators, but they serve different purposes. ConvertKit is a traditional email marketing platform with advanced automation and commerce features, while Substack focuses on monetizing newsletters directly through paid subscriptions. Your choice depends on whether you need sophisticated marketing tools or a simple monetization platform.

comparison_table.tool_b: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, built-in audience

choose_a_if: You need advanced email automation and subscriber tagging for complex marketing workflows
You sell digital products, courses, or want built-in commerce capabilities
You plan to use affiliate marketing and want recurring commissions on your referrals
choose_b_if: You want to monetize your newsletter with zero upfront costs through a revenue-share model
You're a writer prioritizing simplicity and direct audience relationships over marketing complexity
You need unlimited subscribers from day one without hitting platform limits

faq.a: ConvertKit is more feature-rich and better for creators building sophisticated marketing operations, while Substack excels at monetization simplicity for independent writers. Neither is objectively 'better'—it depends on your business model and technical needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Substack?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper if you want to stay free forever using its revenue-share model (taking 10% of subscription revenue). ConvertKit requires a paid plan ($29+/month) for features beyond its 1,000-subscriber free tier, making it more expensive for growing creators.

faq.q: Can I switch from Substack to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists, though the process isn't automatic. You'll need to manually migrate your subscribers and set up new workflows in ConvertKit, but your audience data remains yours to transfer.
